package handler

import (
	"net/http"

	"github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
	"gorm.io/gorm"
	"gorm.io/gorm/clause"
)

// Anti-pattern: Handler builds a FOR UPDATE locking clause directly
// instead of delegating to a repository method that encapsulates locking.

type Wallet struct {
	ID      uint    `gorm:"primaryKey"`
	UserID  uint    `gorm:"column:user_id"`
	Balance float64 `gorm:"column:balance"`
}

func WithdrawHandler(c *gin.Context, db *gorm.DB) {
	userID := c.Param("user_id")

	var wallet Wallet
	db.Clauses(clause.Locking{Strength: "UPDATE"}).
		Where("user_id = ?", userID).
		First(&wallet)

	c.JSON(http.StatusOK, gin.H{"wallet": wallet})
}
